data.title: PayPal Joins Google’s AI-Powered Checkout as Key Payment Option Through Universal Commerce Protocol
data.description:
PayPal To Become Key Payment Option In Google’s New AI-Driven Checkout

PayPal has confirmed it will integrate with Google’s Universal Commerce Protocol (UCP), an AI-powered framework designed to streamline interactions between consumers, retailers, and payment providers.

The company will soon appear as a payment choice within Google’s new checkout system, according to a press statement released over the weekend.

Excited to share @PayPal's support of @Google's Universal Commerce Protocol (UCP) as the next phase of our expanded partnership announced last fall.

This is a milestone moment making agentic commerce a reality for consumers.

Let's go @sundarpichai! 🚀 pic.twitter.com/9QCZo04n67

— Alex Chriss (@acce) January 12, 2026

How Will UCP Change Online Shopping

Google unveiled UCP during the National Retail Federation’s annual convention in New York, positioning it as an open, agentic commerce standard.

The protocol is designed to allow AI systems to assist with product discovery, checkout, and post-purchase support while enabling merchants and payment providers to interact seamlessly.

Vidhya Srinivasan, vice president and general manager of Google Ads & Commerce, explained:

“Instead of requiring unique connections for every individual agent, UCP enables all agents to interact easily.”

The protocol is platform-agnostic, aiming to reduce one-off integrations and support multiple credential providers.

UCP builds on Google’s previous agentic commerce protocols, including Agent Payments Protocol (AP2), Agent2Agent, and Model Context Protocol (MCP), and the company plans to expand its capabilities with loyalty programs, personalised recommendations, and tailored shopping experiences.

We’ve launched the Universal Commerce Protocol (UCP), a new open standard for agentic commerce that works across the shopping journey!

UCP is compatible with A2A, AP2, and MCP, and was co-developed with partners like Etsy, Shopify, Wayfair, and Target → https://t.co/0kpbdUyKj0pic.twitter.com/shuBlZho83

— Google Cloud Tech (@GoogleCloudTech) January 12, 2026

Why PayPal Is Joining UCP

PayPal executives described the protocol as a way to remove barriers that have limited agentic commerce adoption.

Prakhar Mehrotra, SVP and Head of AI at PayPal, said:

“Protocols like UCP turn agentic commerce into something merchants can actually adopt at scale. Interoperability is what allows retailers to connect at once and reach many environments, while maintaining trust, transparency, and control.”

Michelle Gill, general manager of small business and financial services at PayPal, added:

“The next generation of commerce will be defined by how well we build open, trusted infrastructure that serves everyone. Supporting and collaborating with Google on UCP shows how a trusted payments experience layer makes agentic commerce a reality for consumers.”

PayPal has long acted as a reliable infrastructure layer for online payments, connecting hundreds of millions of consumers and tens of millions of merchants globally, while offering fraud protection and buyer-seller safeguards.

The company believes these foundations will be increasingly important as AI agents take on larger roles in shopping.

Who Helped Build UCP

UCP was developed with input from major retailers and e-commerce platforms, including Shopify, Etsy, Wayfair, Target, and Walmart.

AI agents will be a big part of how we shop in the not-so-distant future.

To help lay the groundwork, we partnered with Shopify, Etsy, Wayfair, Target and Walmart to create the Universal Commerce Protocol, a new open standard for agents and systems to talk to each other across… pic.twitter.com/8gDxDWjF9q

— Sundar Pichai (@sundarpichai) January 11, 2026

Vanessa Lee, vice president at Shopify, highlighted the company’s experience in building large-scale checkout systems:

“Shopify has a history of building checkouts for millions of unique retail businesses. We have taken everything we’ve seen over the decades to make UCP a robust commerce standard that can scale.”

Some analysts caution that agentic commerce may not succeed without active coordination between consumers, merchants, and payment providers.

Richard Crone, CEO of Crone Consulting, warned that if checkouts move off merchant sites, “the merchant loses the last touch point…The product detail pages are the fuel that they need to feed their agentic commerce engine.”

PayPal Expands Its European Presence

Alongside its UCP integration, PayPal has participated in a €12 million Series A funding round for Amsterdam-based payments firm Klearly, bringing the company’s total funding to €20 million.

Klearly serves over 4,000 merchants and plans to expand deeper into Italy and Belgium.

A year after €6m seed led via former @Mastercard CEO, Dutch hospitality #payments#startup#Klearly raises €12m Series A led by @PayPal's VC arm. Just 3 years old, offers agile, bar/restaurant focussed #payment layer that is hardware/ePOS agnostic. https://t.co/UPhZ9XeZ1mpic.twitter.com/7Gepx9FxIa

— Rob Fernandes (@rob2775) January 13, 2026

In the United States, PayPal has strengthened its physical presence in New York City with a 10-year lease covering 261,000 square feet at 345 Hudson Street and 555 Greenwich Street in Hudson Square, near Google and Disney offices.

345 Hudson Street (left) and 555 Greenwich Street (right) in Hudson Square

Will UCP Redefine Commerce Infrastructure

PayPal sees UCP as a chance to enhance trust and efficiency in the emerging agentic commerce landscape.

Ashish Gupta, VP/GM of Merchant Shopping at Google, stated:

“For agentic commerce to scale, it’s critical for the industry to align on a common set of standards. We are proud to have PayPal endorse the Universal Commerce Protocol as the foundation for that future.”

With the new integration, AI-driven shopping experiences could become more seamless, giving consumers smarter product suggestions while allowing merchants and payment providers to maintain oversight and reliability.

data.title: Google poached Apple, delivering another crushing defeat for OpenAI.
data.description:
At dawn on January 13th, Beijing time, Apple and Google jointly released a statement announcing a multi-year, in-depth cooperation agreement: the next generation of Apple Foundation Models will be built on Google Gemini models and cloud technology, providing core technical support for the personalized Siri that will be launched this year.

According to the agreement, the cooperation between the two parties will go beyond simple application-layer access.

Google's Gemini model will directly become the building block of Apple Intelligence, especially for complex tasks requiring cloud computing power. Previously, it was reported that users would experience a fully-fledged Siri powered by Gemini in iOS 26.4, which is expected to be released as early as March or April this year. The new Siri will have cross-application information integration capabilities, able to understand screen content, emails, and chat history, truly achieving "understanding what you think." OpenAI: From "Honored Guest" to "Passerby" The most intriguing part of this statement is the name it didn't mention—OpenAI. Back in 2024, when Apple first unveiled Apple Intelligence at WWDC, OpenAI's ChatGPT was its first high-profile partner. In the demonstration at the time, Siri would ask users "Do you want to answer via ChatGPT?" when faced with a difficult question. However, two years later, when Apple decided to build a deeper, next-generation foundational model, it ultimately chose Gemini. This has been interpreted by many overseas media outlets as GPT's "exit," or at least its marginalization. Compared to the "plug-in" service offered by OpenAI, Google's solution this time is more like "internal implantation." By directly using Gemini's model architecture and cloud technology to train Apple's base models, Google successfully penetrated the heart of Apple's ecosystem, much like they did with Samsung. For OpenAI, losing its exclusive access to the iPhone's underlying system is undoubtedly a major setback in the battle for mobile traffic. However, there are two main reasons behind OpenAI's "exit": First, the asymmetry of infrastructure. OpenAI's computing power heavily relies on Microsoft Azure, while Google possesses independent computing power across the entire stack, from TPU chips to data centers. For a giant like Apple with hundreds of millions of users, directly partnering with Google, which possesses underlying computing power, offers significant advantages in terms of cost control and response speed compared to "subcontracting" through Microsoft Cloud via OpenAI. Secondly, from "ally" to "threat." OpenAI is rapidly moving towards hardware. As OpenAI begins developing its own hardware devices and continues to poach talent from Apple's hardware design team (even including the new company of former Apple design director Jony Ive), it is no longer seen by Apple as a pure software service provider, but as a potential and dangerous system-level competitor. The Boomerang of History: From the First iPhone to the Android Wars This collaboration between Apple and Google inevitably evokes memories of the "honeymoon era" of the mobile internet's dawn nearly 20 years ago. In 2007, when the first iPhone was released, Google was Apple's closest ally. The iPhone came pre-installed with native Google Maps and YouTube apps, and Google's then-CEO, Eric Schmidt, even sat on Apple's board of directors. It was a golden age of mutual complementarity: Apple provided revolutionary hardware, and Google provided killer internet services.

But this relationship quickly broke down due to the rise of Android. With Google launching its mobile operating system to compete with the iPhone, Jobs felt deeply betrayed and launched the famous "thermonuclear War," vowing to destroy Android.

For the next decade or so, the two companies clashed fiercely in various battlegrounds, including app stores, map services, and privacy policies. Apple not only removed Google Maps from its pre-installed list but also constantly emphasized "device-side processing" to differentiate itself from Google's "data collection" model.

In the following decade, the two companies fought tooth and nail in various battlegrounds, including app stores, map services, and privacy policies. Apple not only removed Google Maps from its pre-installed list but also constantly emphasized "device-side processing" to differentiate itself from Google's "data collection" model.

data.title: The fifth episode of the second season of "Blockchain Masters" has come to a perfect end.
data.description: On January 8, 2026, the fifth episode of the second season of "Blockchain Masters," the first light-hearted variety show in the Web3 industry, jointly launched by Jinse Finance, Twinkle, and Dashu Finance, and produced by HTX DAO and OracleX, officially went live. As the season finale, this episode brought together 12 guests and attracted over 100 ordinary people to participate on-site, achieving a high level of interaction and participation, thus concluding the second season's journey successfully. Against the backdrop of the ongoing evolution of the Web3 digital wave, "Blockchain Masters" consistently avoids complex jargon and grand narratives, using a light-hearted variety show approach and a lighter, more engaging content design to deeply connect technological concepts with real individuals. Since the project was launched in June 2025, the program has been constantly exploring in terms of content structure and expression. From diverse themes to innovative gameplay, it uses a narrative style that is close to the audience to continuously present the vibrant and diverse aspects of the Web3 world. With the airing of the fifth episode of Season 2, "Operation Disguise," this season has come to a temporary close. Looking back on this journey, "Blockchain Masters" has captured a warmer side of Web3 through authentic interaction and a companion-like experience, leaving room for future content expansion. In the fifth episode of the second season, "Blockchain Masters" further upgraded its format, using innovative mediums such as interactive tasks and character settings to construct a "real, transparent, and user-co-created" Web3 experimental field. Participants are not just guests, and viewers are not just spectators, but become part of the content through interaction, discussion, and consensus. The conclusion of the second season marks an important milestone for "Blockchain Masters" as it enters a new phase. Currently, the program has officially entered the preparation and recruitment stage for the third season. The new chapter will continue the IP's core advantages of being "lightweight, immersive, and therapeutic," while unlocking richer thematic designs, more open participation mechanisms, and deeper user co-creation, continuously exploring more possibilities for Web3 variety show expression. About Us: As a leading global blockchain industry service platform, Jinse Finance covers the entire industry ecosystem, including information, data, research reports, and summits, always staying at the forefront of the industry. This foray into film and television aims to convey the topic of Web3 in popular language and promote its wider dissemination within the industry. Twinkle is an innovative entertainment content platform focusing on Web3 themes. With keen industry insights, it captures the unique pulse of the Web3 world and transforms it into widely appealing entertainment works using professional film and television industry standards. Twinkle believes that entertainment is the best bridge, connecting cutting-edge exploration of Web3 with the curiosity of mainstream audiences. "The Dark Amber: The Crypto Reef" showcases Twinkle's strength in the "Web3 + Suspense Entertainment" direction, aiming to deliver a hardcore yet thrilling audiovisual feast. As a pioneer in Web3 entertainment, Twinkle not only builds platforms but also creates content. Twinkle excels at using professional production processes and creative entertainment techniques to uncover and present compelling stories that embody the core spirit of Web3 (such as decentralization, innovation, and value consensus). It is committed to transforming complex technical issues into captivating and resonant cinematic entertainment experiences. "The Dark Amber: The Crypto Reef" is a high-quality entertainment masterpiece created by Twinkle that blends fintech suspense with deep Web3 thinking, targeting a broader audience. HTX DAO, supported by the HTX exchange and the TRON ecosystem, uses HTX TOKEN as its core credential to build an integrated CeFi+DeFi hub. Adhering to the principles of "co-construction, co-governance, and co-sharing," the community participates in decision-making and shares profits, promoting the development of a free and open crypto financial ecosystem. Tree Finance, using content as a bridge and resources as a link, is committed to promoting the dissemination of blockchain value and providing partners with fertile ground for global exposure and sustainable growth. We focus on providing one-stop solutions for projects, connecting everyone from Web3 startups to every key node in the global crypto ecosystem. OracleX is the world's first decentralized collective consensus platform based on swarm intelligence, redefining the paradigm of human collective intelligence by constructing a next-generation collective intelligence capitalization protocol. OracleX builds a next-generation decision support ecosystem connecting the real world and the digital economy, becoming an indispensable predictive data protocol layer and financial derivative infrastructure in the Web3.0 value internet era. OracleX firmly believes that every contributor to the ecosystem should be a master of the ecosystem, sharing the benefits of platform development. It is not just a prediction platform, but a vibrant "predictive economy." Here, information flows freely, value is distributed fairly, and every participant is a builder and owner of this ecosystem. This is not only an innovation of traditional models, but also a profound exploration of the next generation of internet applications.

data.title: Meta Slashes Metaverse Team by 10% as Zuckerberg Bets Big on AI
data.description:
Meta Prepares For Massive Layoffs At Reality Labs

After burning $70 billion on virtual worlds, Meta signals a dramatic retreat from the metaverse as artificial intelligence takes over the company’s future.

Meta is preparing to cut roughly 10% of its metaverse workforce, dealing a major blow to the division that once defined CEO Mark Zuckerberg’s grand vision of the future. The layoffs, expected to hit Reality Labs, come as Meta aggressively redirects talent and capital toward artificial intelligence, following years of ballooning losses and disappointing user adoption.

BREAKING: META $META PLANNING TO CUT AROUND 10% OF EMPLOYEES IN ITS REALITY LABS DIVISION, PER NYT.

Reality Labs has roughly 15,000 employees, so this could mean ~1,500 layoffs.

Cuts will disproportionately affect those working on VR headsets and the metaverse. Could be… pic.twitter.com/lQArx04Yg7

— WOLF (@WOLF_Financial) January 12, 2026

According to a report by The New York Times, the job cuts could be announced as early as this week, underscoring how quickly Meta’s priorities have shifted. What was once pitched as the next evolution of the internet is now being overshadowed by the explosive rise of AI.

Reality Labs, which employs around 15,000 people, oversees Meta’s virtual and augmented reality ambitions, including Quest VR headsets, Horizon Worlds, and Horizon Workrooms. A 10% reduction would translate to roughly 1,500 job cuts, marking one of the most significant retreats yet from Meta’s metaverse bet.

The move follows mounting evidence that the division has failed to gain meaningful traction. Since its launch in 2020, Reality Labs has racked up more than $70 billion in losses, including $4.4 billion in operating losses in Q3 2025 alone.

We are still profitable after the Meta’s Reality Labs’ losses.

Imagine when we start profiting from it.👀 pic.twitter.com/Kz7ONIQ269

— CHIPCHIPGAME🏆Crypto Poker (@chipchipgame) April 25, 2024

Internally, Meta has already begun reshuffling budgets. Reports in December suggested the company was considering slashing up to 30% of metaverse spending, with those funds instead flowing into AI research, smart wearables, and neural interface technologies, including projects like the Meta Neural Band.

Meta declined to comment on the impending layoffs.

Meta’s pullback marks a stunning reversal from its 2021 rebrand, when the company renamed itself after the metaverse and promised a future built around immersive virtual worlds, digital identities, and Web3 economies.

While gaming-driven platforms like Roblox and Fortnite continue to thrive with hundreds of millions of users, most corporate and blockchain-based metaverses have struggled to attract sustained engagement. Data from DappRadar shows The Sandbox recorded just 776 unique active wallets over the past month, highlighting the gap between early hype and real-world usage.

Meta’s own flagship world, Horizon Worlds, has fared no better. Multiple reports suggest daily active users remain below 1,000, a stark contrast to the scale Zuckerberg once envisioned.

From text prompts to signature styles, creator Metacrafters reveals why Mesh Generation is your go-to generative AI tool for customizing worlds in Meta Horizon. 🛠️🎨

Try it in your mobile or VR world and drop a link! Start building 👉 https://t.co/wcCFHzVlV7

More links 👇 pic.twitter.com/5fCF0vVPHi

— Meta Horizon Developers (@MetaHorizonDevs) December 15, 2025

As enthusiasm faded, investors grew restless — and Meta’s metaverse losses became harder to justify in a market increasingly obsessed with AI.

AI wins as crypto and metaverse ambitions cool

Meta’s retreat from the metaverse mirrors its broader caution around blockchain initiatives. In 2025, shareholders overwhelmingly rejected a proposal to add Bitcoin to the company’s balance sheet, with just 0.08% of votes cast in favor. With Zuckerberg controlling 61% of voting power, the outcome was never in doubt.

🚨LATEST :

Meta Declines Bitcoin for Treasury Reserves #XRP Emerges as a Strategic Alternative 🚀🪙

RETWEET ONLY IF YOU FEEL BULLISH 🚀🪙 pic.twitter.com/ED0uNiXlot

— ToniTheRippler (@thatgirl_chichi) June 1, 2025

The message from investors has been clear: focus on what works.

That focus is now firmly on artificial intelligence. Meta has rapidly expanded investment in generative AI models, recommendation engines, AI-powered assistants, and wearable-integrated intelligence, positioning itself to compete directly with OpenAI, Google, and Anthropic.

After years of chasing virtual worlds that never reached escape velocity, Meta appears to be embracing a more immediate, revenue-driven future. The metaverse may not be dead — but at Meta, it’s no longer the main character.

data.title: The Way of Ether
data.description:
Ethereum itself must pass "walkaway test（walkaway test)". Ethereum's goal is to become the home of trustless and trust-minimized applications, whether in finance, governance, or other fields. It must support applications that are more like tools. Like a hammer, once you buy it, it's yours; not a service-oriented application, whose functionality completely fails once the developers lose interest in maintaining it (or worse, it's hacked). Even if some applications do rely on specific developers, Ethereum should minimize this dependence as much as possible and protect users as much as possible in the event of a dependency failure. However, if the underlying protocol itself depends on a particular "supply provider" for continued availability, even if that "supply provider" is merely part of the overall core developer workflow, then a true application ecosystem cannot be established. The Ethereum blockchain itself must possess the qualities we seek in Ethereum applications. Therefore, Ethereum itself must pass the "walk-out test." This means that Ethereum must reach a state where, if we wish, we can choose a "fixed (ossify) protocol." We don't necessarily have to stop improving the protocol, but we must reach a point where Ethereum's value proposition no longer strictly relies on any features that don't yet exist in the current protocol. This includes at least the following aspects: Complete quantum resistance. We should avoid falling into the trap of postponing quantum resistance to the last minute in order to squeeze out a few more years of efficiency. Individual users can choose this, but the protocol layer should not. Being able to proudly say that the Ethereum protocol, in its current form, will be cryptographically secure for the next hundred years is a goal we should strive to achieve as soon as possible and uphold with pride.

  • Scalable to a sufficiently large architecture. The protocol must be capable of scaling to thousands of transactions per second over time, especially including ZK-EVM verification and data sampling implemented via PeerDAS.. Ideally, future expansion would primarily be achieved through parameter adjustments; even better, these adjustments would not be BPO-style hard forks, but rather, like gas limits, through validator voting mechanisms. This would create a stateful architecture capable of operating for decades. This means we need to decide on and implement some form of partial statelessness and state expiration so that even decades after Ethereum starts running at thousands of TPS, we don't have to worry about synchronization, disk, or I/O demands overwhelming us. It also means forward-looking design for tree structures and storage types for long-term environments.

    • General Account Model (i.e., "Complete Account Abstraction"): Eliminate the hard-coded dependency on ECDSA signature verification in the protocol. We are certain that there is no DoS vulnerability in the pricing system, which includes both the execution layer and the ZK proof layer.

    • A time-tested economic model PoS  Based on our experience over the past five years in Ethereum's Proof-of-Stake (PoS) and even more than a decade earlier, we are confident that this model can remain decentralized for decades to come and support ETH as a trustless collateral asset (e.g., for governance-minimized ETH-collateralized stablecoins). This block-building model, resilient to centralized pressures, ensures censorship resistance even in an uncertain future environment. Ideally, we should complete these difficult but crucial tasks over the next few years, enabling virtually all subsequent innovations to be implemented through client-side optimizations and reflected in the protocol through parameter changes. Each year, we should check off at least one of these, ideally several. Doing things right the first time, based on a full understanding of what is truly right, rather than compromising half-baked fixes, maximizes Ethereum's long-term robustness at both the technical and social levels. Ethereum, all in. This is the way of Ethereum.

data.title: Microsoft Warns U.S. Companies Are Losing Ground As Chinese AI Models Gain Popularity In Emerging Economies
data.description:
China Surges Ahead In AI Race With DeepSeek While U.S. Struggles To Keep Pace

Microsoft warns that Chinese AI companies are rapidly gaining ground globally, using low-cost models and heavy state support to reach markets beyond the West.

Brad Smith, Microsoft president, highlighted DeepSeek’s rapid adoption in emerging markets, noting that China’s open-source models are now competitive and often cheaper than U.S. offerings.

“They benefit from subsidization by the Chinese government. They benefit from subsidies that enable [them] to basically undercut American companies based on price.”

Microsoft is basically sounding the alarm that China is winning the AI game in the parts of the world that matter most, developing countries in Southeast Asia, Africa and Latin America.

They're doing it the smart way too.

They are not trying to build the absolute best AI… https://t.co/5vChebh5vzpic.twitter.com/fgoHRvrFBX

— Milk Road AI (@MilkRoadAI) January 13, 2026

DeepSeek Captures Markets Where U.S. Tech Is Limited

Data from Microsoft shows that DeepSeek’s R1 model, launched last year, has quickly expanded in countries where American firms have limited access.

The company now holds 56% of the AI market in Belarus, 49% in Cuba, and 43% in Russia.

In Africa, it commands 18% of Ethiopia and 17% of Zimbabwe, driven by affordability and open access.

Source: Microsoft

The model’s open-source approach, including free usage and adaptability, has accelerated AI adoption in regions often overlooked by Western companies.

Smith said this growth reflects a broader shift in the global AI landscape.

Is Open Source Outpacing Locked Ecosystems

While U.S. companies like OpenAI, Google, and Anthropic maintain paid, controlled ecosystems, Chinese platforms such as DeepSeek and Meta’s Llama benefit from open-source accessibility.

Smith warned that without significant investment in infrastructure, data centres, and energy support, this divide could deepen global inequality.

“If we rely on private capital flows alone, I don’t think that will be sufficient to compete with a competitor that is subsidised to the degree that Chinese companies often are.”

He called for international development banks and governments to provide funding to support AI adoption in poorer regions.

Today, we released @Microsoft's newest AI Diffusion Report, and I sat down with @BDataScientist, Chief Data Scientist and Director of the AI for Good Lab, for a special edition of #ToolsAndWeapons to unpack the report’s most striking insights. It’s a conversation about… pic.twitter.com/W7IwYF0faP

— Brad Smith (@BradSmi) January 8, 2026

Bright Simons, an AI analyst from Ghana’s IMANI think-tank, said Chinese AI tools are now the go-to for many Africans.

He explained,

“Africans can’t afford very expensive solutions apart from open source, so you have to go to [Meta’s] Llama or Chinese options.”

He also noted local efforts such as Masakhane and InkubaLM, but Chinese models remain dominant in accessibility and reach.

Global AI Use Shows Wide Divide Between North And South

Microsoft’s AI Diffusion Report 2025 shows that 24.7% of people in the global north used AI by the end of 2025, compared with just 14.1% in the global south, leaving a worldwide average of 16.3%.

Source: Microsoft

Smith called the gap a “cause for concern,” warning that without targeted investment, inequality will widen further.

“American companies, as well as Western governments, cannot ignore Africa. If they do, they are closing their eyes to the future of the world more broadly, and I think that would be a grave mistake.”

Strong Models Don’t Guarantee Widespread Adoption

Even in technologically advanced countries, public use of AI does not always match technical capability.

By the end of 2025, about 28% of working-age Americans had used generative AI, placing the U.S. 24th globally.

Meanwhile, South Korea climbed from 25th to 18th, boosted by clearer AI policies, improved language support, and public engagement through cultural trends such as Ghibli-style graphics on social media.

Source: Microsoft

China’s Strategy Focuses On Accessibility And Low Costs

DeepSeek has leveraged its MIT-licensed model and free chatbot to lower both financial and technical barriers.

Adoption in North America and Europe remains limited, but the model has spread quickly across China, Russia, Iran, Cuba, Belarus, and parts of Africa, where usage rates can be two to four times higher than in many Western countries.

Smith emphasised that the U.S. retains advantages in trust and advanced chip access but warned that price competition and global reach are increasingly critical.

DeepSeek’s next major model is expected to launch just before Lunar New Year, signalling continued momentum in regions where American firms have struggled to make inroads.

data.title: Former NYC Mayor Eric Adams Leaves The Office And Started A Crypto Scam Called NYC Token?
data.description:
A post–City Hall crypto debut raises eyebrows

Just weeks after stepping down as mayor of New York City, Eric Adams has returned to the public spotlight with a controversial new crypto project: a Solana-based memecoin called “NYC Token.”

Adams announced the token on Monday through social media posts and a press appearance in Times Square, framing it as a civic-minded blockchain initiative designed to combat antisemitism, address what he described as rising anti-American sentiment, and fund education and scholarships in underserved communities.

“There are two types of Americans — those who live in New York and those who wish they could. If you can’t make it to New York, we’re going to bring New York to you.”

Amid the Times Square sideshows, Eric Adams announces his “NYC Token,” a crypto coin he says will fight antisemitism.

“I’m not taking a salary at this time,” he said of the yet to be launched coin. “Down the line, we will make the determination of doing so” pic.twitter.com/KnTTdTv6y1

— Josie Stratman (@JosieStratman) January 12, 2026

In interviews with FOX Business and local media, Adams said proceeds from the token would be directed to an unnamed nonprofit supporting educational programs, blockchain literacy, and social awareness campaigns. He positioned the project as an extension of his long-standing belief that cities should embrace blockchain technology to drive innovation.

The timing, however, immediately drew scrutiny. Adams officially left office on Jan. 1, and his successor, Zohran Mamdani, has taken a far more skeptical view of crypto and capitalism. Against that backdrop, the sudden launch of a mayor-branded token raised questions about governance, intent, and oversight — questions that intensified within minutes of the token going live.

A violent price swing and on-chain red flags

Despite the high-profile rollout, NYC Token’s market debut unraveled almost immediately. According to DEXScreener, the token fell from roughly $0.47 to around $0.10 within 30 minutes, wiping out hundreds of millions of dollars in paper value as its market capitalization collapsed from nearly $500 million to under $110 million.

A former New York Mayor launched a token called $NYC.

It was marketed as a way to fight “antisemitism and anti-Americanism.”

It reached $600 million.

Here’s what really happened 👇

Eric Adams shared $NYC on Solana, and people assumed it was official. Big name, big city, big… pic.twitter.com/SOnjDwPZeG

— 💎 𝐖𝐋𝐅𝐈 𝐁𝐎𝐒𝐒 💎 (@KekiusElonus) January 13, 2026

Soon after, crypto analysts began flagging suspicious on-chain activity. Rune Crypto reported that wallets linked to the token’s deployment appeared to have removed more than $3.4 million in liquidity near the market peak.

Blockchain analytics firm Bubblemaps echoed those concerns, identifying a wallet connected to the deployer that withdrew roughly $2.5 million in USDC, then later added back about $1.5 million after the price had already dropped more than 60%.

Bubblemaps described the pattern as reminiscent of prior controversial launches where liquidity was heavily manipulated, intensifying accusations that the NYC Token may have been “rugged.” Another analyst warned that the project appeared highly centralized and carried elevated risk.

🚨RUGPULL CALL - #NYC

🎯251: $NYC 3u6u769qdeXf9jk7mw1HQDD7miLxRTCa22S6Y3iJpump

OnChain Checked ✅

⚠️ it's a scam token by @circuz888, bundled and supply control detected.

💀 Team control 35% supply of the token, and split it into 20 different wallets.

15.8% is owned by 12… https://t.co/rINvewlEbcpic.twitter.com/DQxefBdez0

— Devs Hunter (@devs_hunter) January 12, 2026

Neither Adams nor the project’s team has publicly addressed the allegations. Requests for comment from multiple outlets had not received responses at the time of publication.

Opaque tokenomics, political optics, and unanswered questions

Beyond the market turmoil, the project’s lack of transparency has compounded concerns. The NYC Token website provides limited technical detail, with nonfunctional links to both a whitepaper and a purchase page.

While the site claims the project aims to build “a decentralized financial ecosystem as ambitious as the city itself,” it offers no roadmap, governance structure, or named partners.

Tokenomics disclosures have also raised eyebrows. According to project materials cited by multiple outlets, 70% of the total 1 billion token supply is allocated to a centralized “NYC Token Reserve,” with the remaining portion divided among liquidity, development, marketing, and team allocations. Adams has not disclosed the identities of the project’s co-founders and stated that he would not immediately draw a salary from the token.

The controversy stands in sharp contrast to Adams’ record as one of the most pro-crypto mayors in U.S. history. While in office, he accepted his paychecks in Bitcoin and Ether, hosted New York City’s first crypto summit, and promoted initiatives such as NYC Coin and a proposed BitBond — many of which never fully materialized.

Now, as New York enters a new political era under leadership openly critical of crypto, Adams’ memecoin experiment risks becoming a cautionary tale rather than a legacy project. Whether the NYC Token was a rushed civic experiment, a poorly executed launch, or something more troubling remains unclear.

What is certain is that launching a highly speculative token days after leaving public office — only to see it collapse amid rug-pull allegations — has turned a symbolic crypto play into a reputational test, not just for Eric Adams, but for the fragile intersection of politics and memecoin culture.

data.title: $15 billion raised: The transformation of a16z
data.description:
**Note:** a16z recently announced the completion of raising over $15 billion, accounting for 18% of all VC funding raised in the US this year. What is a16z up to? Aaronjmars from Telah VC interprets what a16z is doing and its transformation. Compiled by Jinse Finance, as follows:

a16z's Transformation: From Capital Allocator to Reality Builder

Some fundamental changes are taking place at Andreessen Horowitz. This venture capital firm, which once touted itself as Silicon Valley's premier firm, is transforming into a more ambitious institution—a full-stack coordination engine encompassing technological and political realities.

The most obvious signal came in August 2025 when Alex Danco joined a16z as a contributing editor, responsible for all of the company's written content. This was not a public relations hire. Danco believes that writing is a "power transfer technique"—a mechanism where legitimacy is "co-established" by authors and readers, rather than benevolently granted by institutions. But Danco's appointment is just one small part of a larger plan. In November 2025, a16z released its new media manifesto, revealing an operating model that goes far beyond traditional venture capital. The company now offers a service called "Timeline Takeover"—helping its portfolio companies "win the day's online dominance" by coordinating content across videos, podcasts, articles, and social media. The infrastructure is well-developed. Erik Torenberg leads the new media team, which includes in-house content creators hailed as "online legends," "front-deployed new media" personnel directly stationed at portfolio companies during product launches, and a high-impact talent network ready to amplify chosen narratives. In October 2025, David Booth joined as a partner and head of ecosystems, with a clear focus on building what he calls a "priority connection" mechanism—an infrastructure that directs resources, talent, and attention to a16z portfolio companies rather than competitors. As Marc Andreessen explains, startups need to “enter a virtuous cycle where, as they grow, they accumulate more and more resources… qualified executives, technical staff, future downstream funding, positive brand momentum, public awareness, customers, revenue, and the ability to exert influence in government.” The company will launch an eight-week new media talent development program in January 2026, designed to train operations staff, creators, and storytellers and place them in its portfolio companies. This is not a consulting service, but rather building a complete parallel talent pipeline specifically for the narrative war. The operational capabilities are impressive. The team produces content five times a week across multiple channels, runs an in-house video production department whose members are “trained on set and inspired by new media legends like Mr. Beast,” and maintains what they call “group chats, dinners, events, and a secret network of contacts that helps talented and trustworthy people find each other.” A portfolio company showcased the logical end result: DoubleSpeed ​​is able to use artificial intelligence to control thousands of social media accounts, ensuring they "look as real as possible." Its tagline is: "Never pay real people again." [link to article: https://x.com/aaronjmars/article/1989055799746843063/media/1989051127694786560] This infrastructure development truly began with a16z's $400 million investment in Elon Musk's acquisition of Twitter in 2022—an investment that reportedly lost $288 million by September 2024. However, financial losses don't tell the whole story. Ben Horowitz declared at the time, "Elon is the only person we know, and perhaps the only one in the world, with the courage, talent, and ability to solve all these problems and build the public space we all want and deserve." The company immediately deployed personnel. Sriram Krishnan, a general partner at a16z focused on cryptocurrency, publicly announced that he "will be temporarily helping Elon Musk run Twitter with some other great people," and stated, "I (and a16z) believe this is a very important company that can have a huge impact on the world." Prediction Market Infrastructure Strategy But the media mechanism is only one layer. Danco's article "Prediction: The Successor to Postmodernism" argues that prediction markets represent a fundamental reconstruction of civilization, comparable to modernism and postmodernism themselves. In October 2025, a16z co-led a $300 million Series D funding round for Kalshi, valuing the company at $5 billion. a16z partner Alex Immerman declared that prediction markets "have the potential to become the largest and most important financial market." The company attempted to nominate Brian Quintenz as chairman of the U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC, the agency that regulates forecasting markets). Quintenz is an executive at a16z and a board member of Calshi. However, due to conflicts of interest and opposition from cryptocurrency figures such as the Winklevoss brothers, the White House withdrew Quintenz's nomination in September/October 2025. This failed nomination exposes both the company's ambition to control regulation and its current limitations. From early June 2024 to election week, prediction market trading volume increased 42-fold, with platforms like Polymarket and Kalshi seeing combined monthly trading volumes reaching billions of dollars. During the 2024 election, journalists and Wall Street traders began relying on prediction markets, which themselves "outperformed polls" as a "world-available" signal. When CEOs like Brian Armstrong are influenced by market signals and mention specific cryptocurrencies in their communications with investors, the feedback loop becomes apparent: the market not only predicts, it coordinates.

Even Scott Kominers, a market design expert at a16z, admits that "prediction markets themselves are not always the best way to aggregate information: even for global 'macro' events, they can be unreliable; for more 'micro' issues, the prediction pool may be too small to obtain meaningful signals." But Kalshi's annual trading volume has exceeded $50 billion, growing more than 25 times since the beginning of 2024.

At this scale, the distinction between prediction and coordination disappears. Marc Andreessen endorsed Hillary Clinton in 2016, even tweeting "I support her." But by 2024, his stance had completely reversed. He and Ben Horowitz donated over $5 million to groups supporting Trump, while Andreessen himself donated $33.5 million to political groups supporting cryptocurrency—more than six times the amount he directly donated to Trump. Andreessen explained that Biden's proposed taxation of unrealized capital gains was "the straw that broke the camel's back" because it would force startups to tax their valuation growth. He described the Biden administration as pursuing a "mild authoritarian social revolution" and imposing direct government scrutiny on tech companies. This coordination goes far beyond that. Andresen organized WhatsApp group chats that became "upstream memes of mainstream discourse," acting as "equivalent to underground publications" and helping to facilitate a nationwide "atmosphere shift." These encrypted, ephemeral messaging groups constituted "the dark matter of American politics and media," where "a shocking reorganization toward Donald Trump was shaped and negotiated." Erik Torenberg, the current head of the a16z new media team, played a key role in organizing these communities. This same person, who coordinates the company's "timeline takeover" service, also coordinated the political group chats that shaped the discourse surrounding the 2024 election. The company sees itself as a "legitimacy bank," where founders can "borrow legitimacy or deposit legitimacy." This is not a metaphor. How can their articles be legitimized? In the article, Danco and former Microsoft executive Steven Sinofsky trace the history of legitimacy in the tech industry—from special interest groups in the 1960s, to the “kingmaker” commentaries in PC Magazine in the 1980s, to today’s ecosystem of coordinated influence. The key is: once you’ve established the infrastructure for legitimacy, you’re not selling products, but a vision of the future. As Sinofsky explains, when Microsoft sold products to enterprise customers, “they just wanted to hear my ten-year plan.” This legitimacy stems from the ability to “make compelling predictions about the future.” This is what a16z has built: making certain futures feel inevitable by controlling the infrastructure through which we understand possibilities. In April 2025, a16z officially launched the American Innovators Network with Y Combinator and AI companies, positioning itself as “a small American tech ecosystem leading the next phase of innovation.” Their stance is: "If candidates support a hopeful, technology-enabled future, we support them; if they want to stifle important technologies, we oppose them." See what they've done: Media Infrastructure: Torenberg's new media team provides narrative experts with "timeline takeover as a service," in-house production, and cutting-edge deployment. Talent Pool: Trainees from the New Media Researcher Training Program will be stationed at portfolio companies. Platform Deployment: $400 million invested in Twitter/X, with staff on-site during the transition. Market Infrastructure: A major investor in Kalshi (valued at $5 billion), betting on prediction markets as a coordination mechanism. Coordination Network: WhatsApp groups, dinners, and a "secret network where talented and trustworthy people find each other." Political Stance: Direct ties to the Trump administration, with over $40 million in political spending. Attempts to Exert Regulatory Influence: Quintetz's failed nomination demonstrates both his ambition and his current limitations.

F1 Pit Stop Workers Theory

The company uses Formula 1 racing as a metaphor for itself. General partners are like drivers. But "the outcome of the race was decided years ago by the teams that designed the right chassis, hired the right engineers, trained the mechanics, and built a fanatical fanbase to ensure a steady stream of sponsor funding."

As David Booth writes: "Adrian Newey, while not winning any races, as Red Bull's chief technology officer, transformed this money-burning mid-table team into a world champion team worthy of being recorded in history. And in the next decade, those trendsetting venture capital firms will not only have the best drivers, but also invest carefully in racing."

The machine a16z is building has multiple engines: one that creates legitimacy through coordinated media; one that coordinates funding and attention through predictive markets; one that coordinates political outcomes through encrypted group chats and strategic donations; and one that coordinates talent mobility through scholarship programs and "ecosystem" infrastructure.

What does this mean? When prediction markets are adopted by institutions and integrated with media mechanisms, they cease to be predictive tools. Markets generate "real-time probabilities that are more rigorous than opinion polls, expert commentary, or news headlines"—and when journalists, traders, and executives use these probabilities to make decisions, markets become self-fulfilling prophecies. According to a16z's own definition, "prediction" is becoming a new framework in the postmodern era—a new way of organizing human attention, capital, and action. The company has positioned itself at every crucial juncture. They fund the platforms that set the odds. They employ media teams to decide which issues matter. They organize group chats to coordinate political strategies. They cultivate talent, supplying the next generation of companies. They have attempted (but have not yet succeeded) to place their people in regulatory bodies. This is not a conspiracy theory, but a carefully designed system by those who understand that controlling belief systems is more valuable than controlling production systems. Quintenz's failure to secure a nomination demonstrates that this strategy still has limitations. Opposition from within the cryptocurrency industry, concerns about conflicts of interest, and political complexities may still deter what appears to be overt regulatory capture. But the broader mechanisms continue to operate. New media teams are expanding. Prediction markets are growing. Coordination networks are deepening. The project is beginning to supply portfolio companies with trained narrative experts. The game isn't about predicting the future, but about building the infrastructure to determine which futures are comprehensible, which questions will be asked, and which answers will appear authoritative. a16z is currently openly building this infrastructure, demonstrating remarkable transparency in its operation—while most are still debating whether prediction markets are "more accurate than polls."

data.title: The End of the 1.47 Million Exchange Rate: Iran's Shadow Finance and Digital Counterattack in 2026
data.description:
Author: Trustin

Macro Prelude: From a Currency Ruin of 1.47 Million to Trump's "Rescue Plan"

On January 13, 2026, the air in Tehran was filled with the anxiety following the complete meltdown of the local currency, the Rial. As of today, the Rial's exchange rate against the US dollar has plummeted to an abyss of 1,470,000:1 on the black market. For a country that has been subject to a "Snapback" of UN sanctions since September 2025 and whose official inflation rate has soared to 42.2%, the fiat currency system is not merely devaluing, but a complete collapse of sovereign credit.

President Trump's recent frequent "rescue" signals on Truth Social—including statements like "The United States of America will come to rescue the protesters" and warnings of "Locked and Loaded"—are essentially a targeted attack on the last cornerstone of Iran's sovereign credibility. Geopolitical threads are tightly intertwined around the Strait of Hormuz. This waterway, only about 34 kilometers wide at its narrowest point, carries nearly 20% of the world's crude oil supply. Iranian Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf's statement about "legitimate strikes against US military bases" has pushed energy market panic levels to a fever pitch. It must be understood that the Strait of Hormuz is not only a geopolitical pivot but also Tehran's "energy nuclear weapon." According to shipping data, approximately 84% of the oil transported through this strait is destined for Asia. Iran's strategic logic lies in pressuring the United States by hijacking the engines of the global supply chain (China, India, Japan, and South Korea). Although the United States has achieved energy self-sufficiency, the economic shutdown of its Asian allies would trigger a global financial avalanche, forcing the White House to hesitate in military intervention. Strategic Transformation: From "Tax Avoidance Experiment" to a National-Level "Military Payment Layer" As early as 2020, the Central Bank of Iran (CBI) authorized banks to use regulated mining proceeds to pay for imported goods; in August 2022, Tehran completed its first $10 million cryptocurrency import order. What we will see in early 2026 is this system undergoing a strategic transformation to "full-chain" integration under extreme pressure. On January 2, 2026, Mindex, the export center under the Iranian Ministry of Defense, officially released its settlement terms, allowing buyers to use "digital currency" to pay for export orders of ballistic missiles, drones, and armored vehicles. This marks the establishment of a mature "oil-computing power-military supply" closed loop in Iran, where oil and electricity are converted into computing power, and then computing power is converted into on-chain hard currency. Through small, shell-like VASPs (Virtual Asset Service Providers) registered in the UK and Turkey, Iran's associated shadow banking network currently processes tens of billions of dollars in on-chain funds annually. This sophisticated layering mechanism—a typical 45-day money laundering cycle—utilizes the delays in cross-border supervision to ensure that Tehran's critical military supply chains remain resilient even under physical siege. Digital Rial (CBDC): The "Electronic Shackles" of Sovereign Credit and the Departure of the People Faced with the erosion of asset dollarization brought about by USDT on the Tron network, Tehran accelerated the nationwide rollout of the "Digital Rial" at the end of 2025. However, from a professional compliance perspective, this is not a technological innovation, but a sovereign defense battle in the digital age. The Digital Rial is based on a highly centralized private ledger architecture (similar to Hyperledger), and its core objective is to achieve real-time transparency of every fund flow within the country. During the turmoil in early 2026, Tehran attempted to utilize the programmable nature of the CBDC to implement precise social control—once an address was flagged as "inciting unrest," its account could be locked by the central bank with a single click. However, this attempt is falling into a fatal "trust trap." The public has no confidence in the fiat currency, which is on the ruins of 52% inflation, and the digital currency pegged to the rial is seen as "electronic waste paper" that could depreciate at any time and is completely monitored. This internal lack of trust is having a reverse effect: the forceful push for the digital rial has not only failed to stop capital flight, but has also forced more savings to shift to decentralized, sovereign-free privacy financial networks. Risk Assessment: Physical Blockade and Asymmetric "Algorithmic Poisoning" Under the shadow of military confrontation, as anti-money laundering experts, we are highly vigilant about the complex retaliatory models that Tehran may adopt. This is no longer limited to conventional missiles, but has entered the realm of "asymmetric warfare" where the physical and digital are intertwined. Physical Energy Blackmail: The Noose in the Strait of Hormuz Experts predict that even a single non-lethal attack on merchant ships in the strait will immediately trigger a "war premium" in the global energy market. Oil prices are predicted to instantly break through the $100 mark. This strategy essentially exploits global, especially Asian, energy vulnerabilities to suppress the Trump administration's domestic approval ratings. On-Chain Algorithmic Poisoning and Dust Attacks: This is currently the most covert digital nuclear option. Based on the prototype of the "Tornado Cash dust attack" that appeared in 2022, Tehran is highly likely to launch an "on-chain nuclear proliferation" plan. Iranian agents may use automated scripts to inject contaminated assets (Dust) marked with "terrorist financing" or "sanctioned entities" into tens of thousands of active deposit addresses on major global exchanges in a short period of time. Because global exchanges generally use automated KYT systems and compliance requirements are "better to kill the innocent than let the guilty go free," a large-scale dust injection will induce a massive number of false alarms in the system, resulting in the freezing of the accounts of tens of thousands of innocent users. This artificially created financial liquidity crunch will be Iran's first asymmetric counterattack against Western pressure in the digital realm. Risk Isolation: Implementing "Scalpel-like" Risk Deduction When a large-scale dust attack occurs, TrustIn's core strategy is to implement "isolation of contaminated assets," rather than "account-level complete blocking." We introduce "risk threshold tolerance" and "asset weight analysis." If an exchange account with millions of dollars in compliant transaction records receives only 0.0001 USDT of poisoned funds from a sanctioned address, TrustIn suggests using on-chain traceability technology to virtually isolate this "toxin" at the ledger level. We offer our clients a "compliance deduction" mechanism: the system automatically identifies and records this involuntary receipt of contaminated assets, and sets its weight to zero or a negative value when calculating the overall account risk score. This approach ensures that the exchange maintains normal liquidity, thwarting Tehran's attempt to "self-harm" through Western compliance rules. The "Digital Breakthrough" of Cross-Border CBDC Nodes: With rumors circulating about the digital rial's integration with cross-border settlements in countries like Russia and India, Iran may suddenly switch all large-scale energy contracts to this closed digital clearing system. This is not only to circumvent sanctions but also to establish a parallel clearing network that is completely resistant to Western surveillance. Strategic Forecast: Reconstruction of Financial Order on the Fault Line Standing on the geopolitical fault line of January 13, 2026, the Iranian case proves that in an era of intense competition for digital sovereignty, sanctions themselves are accelerating the emergence of a new kind of "shadow empire" that cannot be completely controlled by a single power. The Trump administration's "rescue plan" faces an "algorithmic adversary" deeply familiar with the weaknesses of digital finance. For TrustIn's clients and global regulators, risk monitoring in 2026 must go beyond simple "geofencing." Compliance teams should focus on on-chain behavioral patterns involving transit countries. Particular vigilance is needed against small-scale, high-frequency "poisoning funds" originating from tagged wallets. In the financial battlefield of 2026, code is sovereignty, and compliance is the defense line. This extreme external pressure is forcing Iran into a "wartime financial mode." In the vacuum created by the complete severing of connections by Western payment giants, Iran did not succumb to paralysis. Instead, it upgraded its five-year experiment in digital asset circumvention into a cornerstone of national survival. On-chain monitoring over the past few years shows that USDT on the Tron network has quietly replaced the rial as the most fundamental form of liquidity in Iranian society. This extreme pragmatism has led Iran to reject the US dollar in political discourse while simultaneously relying on it like never before through digital means at the core of its economy.

data.title: The Future of Cryptocurrency from a Global Perspective
data.description:
Author: Momir Translator: Shan Ouba, Jinse Finance

Previous analyses explored the specific positioning of Bitcoin, Ethereum, and Solana in 2026, with each facing its own opportunities and challenges. However, to truly understand the development trend of cryptocurrencies, we need to take a step back and examine the overall picture from a more macro perspective. The structurally favorable factors supporting the entire development logic of cryptocurrencies are long-term trends with a ten-year time dimension. These macro forces are the foundation upon which all cryptocurrency narratives rely.

The core driver of Bitcoin's continued growth: currency devaluation. Since 2000, gold has yielded an annualized return of approximately 12%, the S&P 500 has yielded approximately 6%, and the annual growth rate of broad money supply (M2) has also remained around 6%. The implications of this data are profound: after adjusting for currency devaluation, the S&P 500 has generated almost no real returns over the past 25 years. In other words, if we use the expansion of the money supply as a benchmark, equity assets are merely a wealth preservation tool. Even so, this preservation effect can only be achieved if 100% of net assets are allocated to this index. This conclusion is the core of the investment logic for non-inflationary assets. As long as major economies continue to rely on the sustained expansion of the money supply, currency depreciation will remain the core driving force for rising non-inflationary asset prices in the long term. Breaking this ties with current economic policies is extremely difficult—after all, there is a lack of motivation for change from all sides: governments lack sufficient discipline to address debt problems; meanwhile, a large amount of power is concentrated in the financial markets, which maximize their own interests through currency depreciation, while the cost is borne by non-investment classes. The global crisis of confidence in fiat currencies: "Capital flows to where it is welcomed and stays where it is treated well." The trend of currency devaluation is directly related to a broader phenomenon: the market's trust in the traditional financial system is declining. Whether for high-net-worth individuals, ordinary citizens, or sovereign nations, cryptocurrencies have become an important tool for hedging against the economic uncertainties of the 21st century. The driving forces behind this trend are converging from multiple dimensions. Capital controls are tightening: Reports of potential capital controls are no longer limited to emerging markets. The UK has proposed a £20,000 cap on stablecoin holdings, and discussions among major economies about restricting capital flows mark the beginning of a new era of financial repression. Early last year, Trump proposed taxing capital outflows from the US—a move that set a rare historical precedent. The weaponization of the financial system: Events such as freezing Russian assets and taking similar measures against the former Venezuelan leadership confirm this decade-long trend. The weaponization of the financial system is accelerating, strongly prompting governments and individuals to seek alternatives to the traditional banking system. The US government is openly exploring various innovative means to pressure adversaries. The more unpredictable the policies of the current dominant financial system, the more urgent the need for market participants to hedge with alternative assets. The choice of hedging tools varies depending on the entity: sovereign states have the ability to stockpile precious metals, thereby breaking their dependence on the existing financial system; while for individuals, Bitcoin is almost the only option. The expansion of the grey economy: Sanctioned countries are increasingly using cryptocurrencies for trade. Examples such as Russia using cryptocurrencies for oil transactions and Iran accepting cryptocurrencies to pay for weapons systems demonstrate the demand-driven application of cryptocurrencies. When traditional transaction channels are blocked, new ones emerge. Damage to the credibility of traditional financial institutions: Criminal investigations of Federal Reserve officials and political interference in central bank personnel appointments have shaken the credibility of the core institutions supporting fiat currency. Once trust is lost, it is difficult to rebuild. Any dysfunction of traditional institutions has proven to be beneficial to the development of crypto assets. The rise of economic populism: Both left-wing and right-wing populist movements share a common characteristic—distrust of the existing financial system. Today, voices questioning the current financial order are emerging on both the left and right wings of the political spectrum: Mandani on the left calls for a "nation without billionaires," while right-wing economic populists advocate "putting banks in trouble," and the center is shrinking. Taxation policies targeting the wealthy: Proposed wealth taxes in several jurisdictions are driving capital flows toward assets that are harder to track and seize. Regardless of the rationality of these policies, their impact on capital flows is predictable. Considering the above factors, it's easy to understand why, in the current era, the product-market fit of such transaction networks—which require no sovereign backing, possess borderless attributes, and have practical use value—is becoming increasingly significant. The End of a 70-Year Trend

The End of a 70-Year Trend

The aforementioned pressures are not merely theoretical; their impact is already reflected in the foreign exchange reserve allocation strategies of central banks and sovereign wealth funds worldwide.

To understand the current situation, perhaps the most relevant macroeconomic chart is the composition of global foreign exchange reserves. In the 70 years following World War II, the US dollar's share of global foreign exchange reserves steadily increased, exceeding 60% at its peak.

However, around 2020, the situation changed. Gold's share of global foreign exchange reserves saw its first increase in seventy years. This marked a fundamental shift: central banks were no longer just talking about reserve diversification, but were taking concrete action. If this trend continues—and geopolitical factors suggest it is highly likely to continue—it will create structural demand for hard currency assets, and Bitcoin is expected to benefit from this. The Triple Positioning of Cryptocurrencies: Digital Gold, Digital Oil, and Digital Dollar With a clearer regulatory framework, it is easier to clarify the core value of cryptocurrencies. Today, the cryptocurrency ecosystem has matured, forming three distinct value propositions that meet different market needs. This framework clearly explains why different crypto assets serve different functions, and why the value of the entire ecosystem is greater than the sum of its parts: Bitcoin anchors the narrative of value storage; Ethereum provides the underlying power for the on-chain real economy; stablecoins build a bridge connecting traditional finance and the crypto world; and decentralized finance (DeFi) constructs the infrastructure for borderless financial services. A Clear and Definite Development Path After clarifying the differentiated positioning of various crypto assets, assessing their future growth potential becomes more intuitive. The two pillars of cryptocurrency development logic—digital gold and the digital economy—both possess enormous room for expansion. Digital Gold Dimension (Bitcoin vs. Gold Market Cap): Bitcoin's current market capitalization is approximately $1.8 trillion, only 6% of gold's total market capitalization of approximately $32 trillion. Even if it only reaches 10%-15% of the market capitalization of gold—a relatively conservative assumption for an asset positioned as "digital gold"—it still represents significant upside potential compared to current prices. The substantial increase in gold prices in 2025 further raises this target market capitalization.

  • Digital Economy Dimension (Stablecoins vs. Broad Money Supply): Stablecoins currently account for approximately 1% of the broad money supply (M2). If this proportion rises to 10%—a level signifying the mainstream application of the digital dollar—the stablecoin market will experience tenfold growth. Related infrastructure is under rapid construction, and the market focus has shifted to: how quickly will user adoption occur?

  • Digital Economy Dimension (Stablecoins vs. Broad Money Supply): Stablecoins currently account for approximately 1% of the broad money supply (M2). If this proportion increases to 10%—a level that signifies the mainstream application of digital dollars—the stablecoin market will experience tenfold growth. Related infrastructure is being built at an accelerated pace, and the market's focus has shifted to: How fast will user adoption be?

    • Cryptocurrencies serve both ends of the market: on the one hand, developed economies view alternative financial networks as a tool to hedge against risks in the current financial system; on the other hand, the current financial system's leaders are gradually sending positive signals to cryptocurrencies because they need new buyers of the US dollar and dollar-denominated debt—especially given the current reversal in the long-term trend of the US dollar.

    We expect the market capitalization of stablecoins to exceed one trillion US dollars within the next decade. The United States is gradually realizing the two major strategic values ​​of stablecoins:

    1. Debt financing function: Stablecoin issuers must hold reserve assets, which are usually mainly US Treasury bonds. Every dollar of stablecoins issued corresponds to an additional rigid demand for US Treasury bonds.

    2. Continuing the Dollar's Hegemony: The digital dollar extends the influence of the US currency beyond the traditional banking system. Against the backdrop of a threatened dollar dominance, stablecoins offer the US a new path to maintain its financial influence.

    Relevant data has confirmed this trend: TEDA currently holds approximately $135 billion in US Treasury bonds, ranking 17th globally among holders of US Treasury bonds, exceeding the holdings of Germany, the UAE, and Saudi Arabia.

    We believe that stablecoin issuers are likely to soon become the largest creditors of the US government.

    We believe that stablecoin issuers are likely to soon become the largest creditors of the US government.

  • DTCC No-Action Letter (December 2025): The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission approved this custodian institution, which manages over $100 trillion in assets, to conduct on-chain tokenization of stocks, bonds, and Treasury bonds.

    • Core Conclusion: All valuable assets will eventually be on-chain.

    Two Major Entry Points to Financial Popularization: Super Apps and Asset Tokenization

    Favorable macroeconomic trends and a gradually clarifying regulatory framework have laid the foundation for industry development, but user adoption still requires specific channels. The next wave of cryptocurrency growth will be driven by two complementary forces.

    1. Technology Giants Attract New Users: Technology giants will play a key role in the popularization of cryptocurrencies.

      2. Technology Giants Attract New Users: Technology giants will play a key role in the popularization of cryptocurrencies.

      Key Opinion: All Valuable Assets Will be on-chain.

      Technology Giants Attract New Users:
